data.content:
### PATCH /v9/projects/{id} - Update Project Password Protection Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployment-protection/methods-to-protect-deployments/password-protection This endpoint allows you to enable, update, or disable password protection for a specific Vercel project. You can define the scope of protection (e.g., production, all, or preview deployments) and set or remove the password. ```APIDOC ## PATCH /v9/projects/{id} ### Description Updates the password protection settings for an existing Vercel project. This includes enabling, modifying, or disabling password protection for various deployment types. ### Method PATCH ### Endpoint `/v9/projects/{id}` ### Parameters #### Path Parameters - **id** (string) - Required - The ID or name of the project to update. #### Request Body - **passwordProtection** (object | null) - Required - An object defining password protection settings, or `null` to disable password protection. - **deploymentType** (string) - Required (if `passwordProtection` is an object) - Specifies which deployments are protected. Allowed values: `prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews` (Standard Protection), `all` (All Deployments), `preview` (Only Preview Deployments). - **password** (string) - Required (if `passwordProtection` is an object) - The password to set for protection. ### Request Example ```json // Enable / Update Password Protection { "passwordProtection": { "deploymentType": "prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews", "password": "" } } // Disable Password Protection { "passwordProtection": null } ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **id** (string) - The unique identifier of the updated project. - **name** (string) - The name of the updated project. - **passwordProtection** (object | null) - The updated password protection settings for the project, or `null` if disabled. - **deploymentType** (string) - The type of deployments protected. - **protectionDomain** (string) - The domain used for password protection. #### Response Example ```json { "id": "prj_xxxxxxxxxxxx", "name": "my-vercel-project", "passwordProtection": { "deploymentType": "prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews", "protectionDomain": "my-vercel-project.vercel.app" } } ``` ``` -------------------------------- ### Deploy to Production with Vercel CLI Source: https://vercel.com/docs/custom-environments This Vercel CLI command initiates a deployment directly to the production environment. It bypasses automatic preview deployments and ensures the latest changes are live, typically used for explicit production releases. ```bash vercel --prod ``` -------------------------------- ### Configure Vercel Password Protection via API Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployment-protection/methods-to-protect-deployments/password-protection This JSON payload is used with the Vercel API to enable, update, or disable password protection for a project. It allows specifying the deployment types to protect ('prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews', 'all', 'preview') and the password. Setting 'passwordProtection' to 'null' disables the feature. ```json // enable / update password protection { "passwordProtection": { "deploymentType": "prod_deployment_urls_and_all_previews" | "all" | "preview", "password": "" } } ``` ```json // disable password protection { "passwordProtection": null } ``` -------------------------------- ### Configure Vercel GitHub Auto-Aliasing for Preview Deployments in vercel.json Source: https://vercel.com/docs/project-configuration/git-configuration Setting `github.autoAlias` to `false` in `vercel.json` means Vercel for GitHub will create preview deployments upon merge, rather than automatically aliasing to production. It is recommended to follow the staged production build workflow instead of relying solely on this setting for production promotion. ```json { "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json", "github": { "autoAlias": false } } ``` -------------------------------- ### Bypass Deployment Protection for a Single Request Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation This method describes how to send a request to your Vercel deployment, overriding any active Deployment Protection (Password Protection, Vercel Authentication, or Trusted IPs) by including a generated automation bypass secret in the request headers or as a query parameter. ```APIDOC ## ANY /your-protected-deployment-path ### Description Send a request to a protected Vercel deployment, bypassing its security measures using a generated automation bypass secret. This can be achieved using either an HTTP header (recommended) or a query parameter. ### Method ANY (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.) ### Endpoint /your-protected-deployment-path (Conceptual endpoint representing any path on your Vercel deployment) ### Parameters #### Query Parameters - **x-vercel-protection-bypass** (string) - Required - The secret generated in your Vercel project settings to bypass deployment protection. Example: `your-generated-secret` #### Request Headers - **x-vercel-protection-bypass** (string) - Required - The secret generated in your Vercel project settings to bypass deployment protection. This method is strongly recommended over using a query parameter. Example: `your-generated-secret` ### Request Example ```http GET /your-app/dashboard?x-vercel-protection-bypass=your-generated-secret HTTP/1.1 Host: your-deployment.vercel.app # OR (Recommended using header) GET /your-app/dashboard HTTP/1.1 Host: your-deployment.vercel.app x-vercel-protection-bypass: your-generated-secret ``` ### Response #### Success Response (2xx) - **Deployment Content** (html/json/etc.) - The expected content of your deployment as if protection was not active. The specific response structure depends on your application. #### Response Example ```html Your Dashboard

Welcome to your protected application!

You have successfully bypassed protection.

``` ``` -------------------------------- ### Deploy Vercel Project to Production via CLI (terminal) Source: https://vercel.com/docs/cron-jobs/quickstart This command-line instruction deploys your Vercel project to its production environment. Deploying to production is essential for activating and running Vercel cron jobs, as they are not executed in preview deployments. ```terminal vercel deploy --prod ``` -------------------------------- ### Configure Vercel Git Deployment for Specific Branches in vercel.json Source: https://vercel.com/docs/project-configuration/git-configuration This configuration disables automatic deployments for the 'dev' branch. By default, any branch not explicitly mentioned will have deployments enabled, allowing granular control over which branches trigger new deployments. ```json { "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json", "git": { "deploymentEnabled": { "dev": false } } } ``` -------------------------------- ### Disable Vercel Git Deployments for Branches Matching Minimatch Pattern in vercel.json Source: https://vercel.com/docs/project-configuration/git-configuration This `vercel.json` configuration uses minimatch syntax to prevent automatic deployments for any branch that starts with 'internal-'. This allows for flexible control over deployment triggers based on common branch naming conventions, such as feature or internal development branches. ```json { "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json", "git": { "deploymentEnabled": { "internal-*": false } } } ``` -------------------------------- ### Disable Vercel Toolbar for a Specific Branch using .env Source: https://vercel.com/docs/vercel-toolbar/managing-toolbar To disable the Vercel Toolbar for a particular preview branch, set this environment variable to '0'. This hides the toolbar for that branch, preventing it from appearing. ```env VERCEL_PREVIEW_FEEDBACK_ENABLED=0 ``` -------------------------------- ### Promote Vercel Deployment to Production (Go & TypeScript) Source: https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/reference/endpoints/projects/points-all-production-domains-for-a-project-to-the-given-deploy This snippet demonstrates how to promote an existing Vercel deployment to production using the `requestPromote` method. It requires authentication via a Vercel bearer token and specifies the `projectId` and `deploymentId` as path parameters, with optional `teamId` and `slug` for team-specific operations. ```go package main\n\nimport(\n\t"os"\n\t"github.com/vercel/vercel"\n\t"context"\n\t"log"\n)\n\nfunc main() {\n s := vercel.New(\n vercel.WithSecurity(os.Getenv("VERCEL_BEARER_TOKEN")),\n )\n\n ctx := context.Background()\n res, err := s.Projects.RequestPromote(ctx, "", "", nil, nil)\n if err != nil {\n log.Fatal(err)\n }\n if res != nil {\n // handle response\n }\n} ``` ```typescript import { Vercel } from "@vercel/sdk";\n\n const vercel = new Vercel({\n bearerToken: "",\n });\n\n async function run() {\n await vercel.projects.requestPromote({\n projectId: "",\n deploymentId: "",\n teamId: "team_1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j1k2l",\n slug: "my-team-url-slug",\n });\n\n\n }\n\n run(); ``` -------------------------------- ### Configure Playwright for Vercel Protection Bypass Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation This configuration snippet for Playwright demonstrates how to set HTTP headers to bypass Vercel deployment protection. It uses the `VERCEL_AUTOMATION_BYPASS_SECRET` environment variable for the bypass secret and optionally sets a cookie for persistent bypass during in-browser testing. ```TypeScript const config: PlaywrightTestConfig = { use: { extraHTTPHeaders: { 'x-vercel-protection-bypass': process.env.VERCEL_AUTOMATION_BYPASS_SECRET, 'x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie': true | 'samesitenone' (optional) } } } ``` -------------------------------- ### Set Bypass Cookie for Follow-up Requests Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation This method allows you to set a cookie that enables subsequent requests from the same client to the deployment to bypass protection without needing to include the `x-vercel-protection-bypass` header or query parameter in every request. This is particularly useful for in-browser testing scenarios. ```APIDOC ## ANY /your-protected-deployment-path ### Description Initiate a request to a protected Vercel deployment that also instructs Vercel to set a bypass cookie. This cookie then allows all subsequent requests from the same client to bypass protection for a limited duration. ### Method ANY (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.) ### Endpoint /your-protected-deployment-path (Conceptual endpoint representing any path on your Vercel deployment) ### Parameters #### Query Parameters - **x-vercel-protection-bypass** (string) - Required - The generated secret to bypass initial deployment protection. Example: `your-generated-secret` - **x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie** (string) - Optional - Set to `true` to enable cookie-based bypass with default `SameSite: Lax`. Set to `samesitenone` to use `SameSite: None`, which is required for cross-site contexts like iframes. #### Request Headers - **x-vercel-protection-bypass** (string) - Required - The generated secret to bypass initial deployment protection. (Recommended) Example: `your-generated-secret` - **x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie** (string) - Optional - Set to `true` or `samesitenone` as described for query parameters. (Recommended) ### Request Example ```http GET /your-app/login?x-vercel-protection-bypass=your-generated-secret&x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie=true HTTP/1.1 Host: your-deployment.vercel.app # OR (Recommended using headers) GET /your-app/login HTTP/1.1 Host: your-deployment.vercel.app x-vercel-protection-bypass: your-generated-secret x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie: samesitenone ``` ### Response #### Success Response (2xx) - **Deployment Content** (html/json/etc.) - The expected content of your deployment. - **Set-Cookie Header** - An additional `Set-Cookie` header will be present in the response to establish the bypass cookie on the client side. #### Response Example ```http HTTP/1.1 200 OK Content-Type: text/html Set-Cookie: __vercel_deployment_bypass=...; Path=/; Expires=...; HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax Login Page

Welcome!

Cookie set for future bypass.

``` ```
